visual_attributes.morphology: The body is divided into three main parts: cephalothorax, abdomen, and telson.
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Cephalothorax is the largest part, followed by the abdomen and the telson.

domain_specific_visual.EYES: Compound and simple eyes located on the cephalothorax
domain_specific_visual.LIMBS: Multiple pairs of legs attached to the cephalothorax

functional_attributes.primary_function: Ecological role as a benthic organism

domain_specific_functional.BLOOD: Contains Limulus amebocyte lysate used to detect bacterial endotoxins
domain_specific_functional.REPRODUCTION: Females lay eggs on sandy beaches

relationships.description: Horseshoe crabs are more closely related to arachnids than to crustaceans.

relationships.target: Atlantic coast of North and Central America
relationships.description: The Atlantic horseshoe crab is native to this region.

contextual_attributes.origin: Triassic period, approximately 250 million years ago
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Eastern coast of North and Central America, South, Southeast, and East Asia
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Considered a living fossil due to minimal evolutionary change
